source_intake_record.raw_question_text: As shown in the figure, Master Zhao uses a piece of triangular tinplate to cut out a piece of square tinplate for reserve. In $\triangle ABC$, $BC=120$, the height $AD=80$, the square $EFGH$ has its side $GH$ on side $BC$, and $E, F$ are respectively on sides $AB, AC$. Then, what is the length of the side of the square $EFGH$?
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text:
\textbf{Solution:} Set the side length of the square part as $x$.\\
In square $EFGH$, $EF\parallel BC$, $EH\parallel AD$\\
$\therefore \angle AEF=\angle ABC$, $\angle EAF=\angle BAC$; $\angle BHE=\angle BDA$, $\angle B=\angle B$\\
$\therefore \triangle AEF\sim \triangle ABC$, $\triangle BEH\sim \triangle BAD$\\
$\therefore \frac{EF}{BC}=\frac{AE}{AB}$, $\frac{EH}{AD}=\frac{BE}{BA}$\\
$\therefore \frac{EF}{BC}+\frac{EH}{AD}=\frac{AE}{AB}+\frac{BE}{BA}=1$\\
$\therefore \frac{x}{120}+\frac{x}{80}=1$ \\
Solving for $x$ gives: $x=\boxed{48}$

issues: metadata.image_paths contains `52601760.png`, which does not match the provided artifact image path.

image_dependency: The question explicitly says "As shown in the figure" and depends on the placement of square `EFGH` on triangle `ABC`.
The required reasoning uses geometric/spatial relations (`E` on `AB`, `F` on `AC`, `GH` on `BC`) that are naturally tied to the diagram.
multi_step: Solving requires combining multiple geometric facts: identifying parallel lines from the square, establishing triangle similarity, forming an equation, and solving for the side length.
The provided answer uses at least two linked similarity arguments (`triangle AEF ~ triangle ABC` and `triangle BEH ~ triangle BAD`) before the final computation.
This is not a direct lookup; it requires structured geometric reasoning from the figure and given lengths.
verifiability: The target answer is a single numeric quantity (the side length of the square), which is straightforward to check.
The provided solution exposes an explicit equation, `x/120 + x/80 = 1`, yielding `x = 48`, so intermediate steps are rule-checkable.
`registry_passed` is true, and both question and answer are present in the record.

normalization_record.normalized_answer_text:
\textbf{Solution:} Set the side length of the square as $x$.\\
In square $EFGH$, $EF\parallel BC$, $EH\parallel AD$.\\
$\therefore \angle AEF=\angle ABC$, $\angle EAF=\angle BAC$; $\angle BHE=\angle BDA$, $\angle B=\angle B$.\\
$\therefore \triangle AEF\sim \triangle ABC$, $\triangle BEH\sim \triangle BAD$.\\
$\therefore \frac{EF}{BC}=\frac{AE}{AB}$, $\frac{EH}{AD}=\frac{BE}{BA}$.\\
$\therefore \frac{EF}{BC}+\frac{EH}{AD}=\frac{AE}{AB}+\frac{BE}{BA}=1$.\\
$\therefore \frac{x}{120}+\frac{x}{80}=1$.\\
Solving gives $x=\boxed{48}$.

\textbf{Solution:} Set the side length of the square as $x$.\\
In square $EFGH$, $EF\parallel BC$, $EH\parallel AD$.\\
$\therefore \angle AEF=\angle ABC$, $\angle EAF=\angle BAC$; $\angle BHE=\angle BDA$, $\angle B=\angle B$.\\
$\therefore \triangle AEF\sim \triangle ABC$, $\triangle BEH\sim \triangle BAD$.\\
$\therefore \frac{EF}{BC}=\frac{AE}{AB}$, $\frac{EH}{AD}=\frac{BE}{BA}$.\\
$\therefore \frac{EF}{BC}+\frac{EH}{AD}=\frac{AE}{AB}+\frac{BE}{BA}=1$.\\
$\therefore \frac{x}{120}+\frac{x}{80}=1$.\\
Solving gives $x=\boxed{48}$.

variants.title: MM-Math 开放题
variants.rewritten_question_text: As shown in the figure, Master Zhao uses a piece of triangular tinplate to cut out a piece of square tinplate for reserve. In $\triangle ABC$, $BC=120$, the height $AD=80$, and square $EFGH$ has side $GH$ on side $BC$, with $E$ and $F$ respectively on sides $AB$ and $AC$. What is the side length of square $EFGH$?
variants.expected_answer:
\textbf{Solution:} Set the side length of the square as $x$.\\
In square $EFGH$, $EF\parallel BC$, $EH\parallel AD$.\\
$\therefore \angle AEF=\angle ABC$, $\angle EAF=\angle BAC$; $\angle BHE=\angle BDA$, $\angle B=\angle B$.\\
$\therefore \triangle AEF\sim \triangle ABC$, $\triangle BEH\sim \triangle BAD$.\\
$\therefore \frac{EF}{BC}=\frac{AE}{AB}$, $\frac{EH}{AD}=\frac{BE}{BA}$.\\
$\therefore \frac{EF}{BC}+\frac{EH}{AD}=\frac{AE}{AB}+\frac{BE}{BA}=1$.\\
$\therefore \frac{x}{120}+\frac{x}{80}=1$.\\
Solving gives $x=\boxed{48}$.
